mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which increases corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 24-month Spearman correlation between INDPRO monthly change and subsequent 20-day SPX return is less than 0.1 or negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a critical threshold of 7.0% after remaining above it for at least 3 months, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops below 7.0% after being above for at least 3 months
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE is below 7.0%, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and housing market strength, which jointly signal economic momentum and lead to positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon. The interaction amplifies the predictive power beyond either series individually.
failure_condition: If the composite z-score of HOUST and RSAFS monthly changes fails to predict 5-day returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a very low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 0.5%), a further decline in the federal funds rate signals additional monetary accommodation that compresses discount rates, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS decreases in the low-rate regime are not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p > 0.10) over the available sample.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 600,000 units after being below that level for at least 2 months, it signals a strong housing market recovery that boosts construction employment and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 600 (thousands of units) after being below for at least 2 months
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns following threshold breach are not positive in at least 55% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms broad economic strength from both labor and production sides, making the joint signal more reliable than either series alone and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the average of standardized PAYEMS and INDPRO growth rates has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in retail sales exceeding 0.5% indicates strong consumer spending that directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns following the trigger are not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is below its 2017 baseline level of 100, a rise in the index signals manufacturing recovery from a weak base, which has a stronger positive effect on S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon than in normal production regimes.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following INDPRO gains in the low-production regime (INDPRO < 100) are not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p > 0.10).
